key_insight: Increasing the barrel filling ratio from 43% to 53% made detonation-sprayed Cr3C2–NiCr coatings denser and improved wear resistance, even though friction increased.
engineering_relevance: This suggests barrel filling ratio is a practical process lever for tuning coating integrity and tribological durability in wear-critical components.
method: Detonation-sprayed Cr3C2–NiCr coatings at 43% and 53% barrel filling ratios under identical spraying conditions.
Compared microstructure and phase composition using microscopy and X-ray diffraction.
Evaluated dry-sliding tribology with ball-on-disc tests at 10 and 15 N and 5 and 10 cm/s.
evidence_signal: The 53% coating showed the lowest wear rate, reaching 1.23 × 10^-4 mm3/(m·N), alongside reduced porosity and better structural integrity.
watch_item: Higher barrel filling ratio improved wear resistance here, but the higher friction coefficient indicates a trade-off that may matter in low-friction applications.
paper_title: Effect of Barrel Filling Ratio on the Microstructure, Phase Composition and Tribological Performance of Detonation-Sprayed Cr3C2–NiCr Coatings
